general_principles: This is a publicly-shipped SAMPLE playbook: a clearly fictional 'Sample Agreement' used for demos, screenshots, and documentation so no real playbook is ever exposed by accident. Default toward ACCEPT for anything that doesn't touch a hard-rejection floor below.
Push back only when a counterparty change would remove a floor protection this playbook relies on, or introduce a term this playbook rejects outright.
Be decisive. Every change is either ACCEPT (apply the change) or REQUEST_CHANGE (counter with specific replacement text and a one- or two-sentence footnote rationale). Never 'flag for review'.
Identify each issue by section number and title from the proposed document.
Never invent obligations that aren't in this playbook. Never accept obligations that aren't in this playbook unless they are de minimis.

accept_when: Change is clerical, stylistic, or grammatical without legal impact.
Change updates the counterparty's own legal name, address, contact info, signatory, or notice recipient.
Change is within an explicit acceptable_variations range defined in this playbook for the relevant topic.
Change falls inside any de_minimis_categories entry below.
Change is mutual and symmetric (e.g., adding a clarification that applies to both parties).
request_change_when: Change matches an entry on the hard_rejections list.
Change removes a floor protection this playbook relies on (the sample delivery cap, the liability cap, etc.).
Change creates new liability, risk, or expense not contemplated by this sample playbook.

topics.our_standard: Each party may request up to 10 free samples per calendar quarter on a non-exclusive basis; the recipient bears its own evaluation costs and neither party owes the other a purchase commitment.
must_preserve: mutual non-exclusivity of the sample arrangement
reject_if_proposed: perpetual exclusivity over sample formulas or designs

topics.our_standard: $10,000 mutual aggregate liability cap for any claim arising out of a sample; mutual waiver of consequential, special, punitive, and indirect damages.

hard_rejections.description: Counterparty introduces perpetual or exclusive rights over our sample formulas or designs.
Counterparty removes or alters the mutual $10,000 aggregate liability cap for samples.

de_minimis_categories: Counterparty's own legal name, address, email, or signatory updates.
Restating existing obligations in different words.

footnote_phrasing_rules: One or two sentences per footnote. No more.
Name the specific risk created rather than generic phrases like 'not standard'.
Plain English. Avoid legalese where a plain word does the job.
State the position. Don't hedge with 'we generally prefer' or 'our usual practice is'.

system_status.manual_review_routing: Manual-review states are operational statuses and never external legal decisions.

anchor_migrations.reason: CI drift-gate fixture (same fixture shape used by every precision-profile playbook -- see tests/anchor/test_heading_hash_drift.py): simulates a §8/§9 renumbering so the drift gate has a covering migration record to check against for this playbook, independent of this sample form's own real section layout.
CI drift-gate fixture (see sec-8 entry above for the shared rationale).
